title: Seed product detail and variant data
verify: Seeded product data includes variant dimensions for color, size, length, material, and applicable related attributes.
Seeded data includes at least one unavailable or out-of-stock variant and one product with no variants or a single default sellable SKU.
Product detail persistence smoke reads products, option dimensions, variants/SKU, prices, and availability through the backend/Medusa boundary.

verify.summary: Functional verification gates passed: db:seed produced local non-production product detail seed data and smoke:product-detail proved multi-option variants, unavailable variant, default SKU product, SKU, price, option dimensions, and availability through backend/PostgreSQL. Memory Bank lint passed. Closure is not eligible because implementation changed apps/backend/package.json to expose the required smoke:product-detail npm script while TASK-010 runtime_context.allowed_write_scope and packet scope omit apps/backend/package.json. After the verification verdict was recorded, strict mb-doctor reports TASK-010 packet source_task_hash stale, so packet refresh is also required before closure.

TASK-010 verification passed after scope reconciliation. TASK-010 touched_files and runtime_context.allowed_write_scope now include apps/backend/package.json, matching the required smoke:product-detail npm gate. Packet was refreshed before reverify, db:seed passed with local non-production seed data, smoke:product-detail proved multi-option variants, unavailable variant, default SKU product, SKU, price, option dimensions, and availability through backend/PostgreSQL, and Memory Bank lint passed. T2 manual closure criteria are satisfied; feature-level FT-002 semantic completion remains pending after all FT-002 tasks.

evidence_required: Summary of seeded products, option dimensions, variants/SKU, prices, and availability cases.
constraints: Product and variant source of truth is Medusa backend/PostgreSQL.
Seed data must be local/test data, not production data.
Seed data must support product detail and variant selection verification without implementing durable cart behavior.
invariants: No production secrets or production data may be committed.
Do not create inventory reservation, order, payment, or durable cart state in FT-002.
purpose: Provide backend-owned product detail and variant data needed to verify variant-aware product cards and product detail selection.
success_outcome: Local backend data can prove FT-002 product detail and variant selection behavior through repeatable seed and smoke commands.
anti_goals: Do not implement durable cart persistence or cart merge.
Do not hardcode product variant data in the storefront as source of truth.
Do not implement inventory reservation or order behavior.

stop_conditions: Seed path cannot write/read through the backend persistence boundary.
Variant data model contradicts the FT-002 tech spec.
